metadata.title:
LIQUOR men TRIUMPH
IN THE CITY COUNCIL

pages.raw_text:
police department endorsement,
showed a new side to his get up last
night when he said the license COM
mitttee of this council was not gov
erned by any resolution that passed
the preceding council. The remark
followed query by Councilman
Pratt as to whether or not the license
committee has required applicants
for licenses TO present recommenda-
tions from the police department,
pursuant to a resolution offered by
him and adopted by the council pre-
ceding the present council. 1t devel-
oped that the license committee has
required no such Recommendation.


In all likelihood new ordinance
will be drafted by Mr.. Pratt requir-
Ing the removal Of boxes from all
saloons. This is in strict conformity
with the reform plank in the repub-
lican platform on which Mayor Boyd
was elected. The republican party
has always taken much pride In liv-
ing up to it's party pledges, and there
Is no doubt that such an ordinance
will meet the happy and consistent
support of Mayor Boyd and of the
four republicans who are members
of the city council.
